skill.name: clinic-research-design
skill.description: Generates a structured prompt framework for clinical study protocols. Supports Diagnostic, Efficacy, Etiology, and Prognosis studies. Calculates sample size and provides logic guides for LLMs.
skill.summary: Generates structured clinical protocol frameworks with sample size calculations and AI writing guidance for four study types.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: All 129 static findings were evaluated and dismissed as false positives. The 100+ 'Weak cryptographic algorithm' alerts are caused by Python exponentiation operator (**) used in standard statistical sample size formulas, not cryptographic operations. The 37 'shell backtick execution' findings are bash command examples inside markdown code blocks (SKILL.md), not active code. The 'Windows SAM database' false positive triggered on an HTML comment tag. The heuristic combination warnings incorrectly assumed credential access exists. The only real behavior is local filesystem writes to an output directory, which is the intended protocol document generation. No malicious intent, credential access, network calls, or unsafe code execution was found.

high_findings.title: Mathematical exponentiation mistaken for weak cryptographic algorithm
high_findings.description: Static scanner flagged Python exponentiation operator (**) as 'Weak cryptographic algorithm' across 100+ locations. All instances are statistical formulas for sample size calculation (e.g., z_alpha**2, n**2 in sample_size.py), markdown bold formatting in SKILL.md, or data values in JSON. No actual cryptographic operations exist. This is a systematic false positive.

medium_findings.title: Markdown code blocks flagged as shell execution
medium_findings.description: 37 findings flagged backtick commands in SKILL.md as Ruby/shell execution. All instances are bash command examples inside markdown code blocks (e.g., 'python scripts/main.py --help'). These are usage documentation, not active executable code. No shell injection or arbitrary execution risk exists.

medium_findings.title: Filesystem write operations flagged
medium_findings.description: os.makedirs() and open().write() in main.py:75-82 create an output directory and write the generated protocol markdown file. This is the intended core functionality of the skill. File paths are constructed from the skill root directory and an output subdirectory. No arbitrary file write, path traversal, or malicious file modification risk.

low_findings.title: System reconnaissance false positive on audit metadata
low_findings.description: Scanner flagged 'System reconnaissance' at audit_result_v2.json:27 which is the practice_boundaries meta field, and SKILL.md:46 which is output discipline documentation. Neither relates to system reconnaissance.

low_findings.title: Source attribution URL flagged as hardcoded network
low_findings.description: Two findings flagged a URL on SKILL.md:7 as 'Hardcoded URL'. The URL is a citation link to the source GitHub repository (https://github.com/aipoch/medical-research-skills). This is standard source attribution and does not indicate API calls, data exfiltration, or unauthorized network activity.

content.user_title: Design Clinical Research Protocols with Structured Prompts
content.value_statement: Researchers struggle to create consistent clinical study protocols that follow ICH-GCP standards. This skill generates structured protocol frameworks with pre-calculated sample sizes and section-specific writing guidance for AI agents and medical writers.
actual_capabilities: Generates structured clinical study protocol outlines with LLM writing instructions
Calculates sample size for diagnostic, efficacy, etiology, and prognosis studies
Classifies study types automatically from PICO (Population, Intervention, Comparator, Outcome) inputs
Produces section-by-section writing guidance for medical writers and AI agents
Supports multiple study designs including RCT, cohort, diagnostic accuracy, and single-arm trials
Creates output in markdown format for further editing and refinement
limitations: Does not generate final clinical documents, only structured outlines with writing instructions
Sample size calculations require manually provided statistical parameters and assumptions
Does not replace formal ethics committee review or regulatory submission requirements
Protocol templates use placeholder identifiers that must be customized for each study

use_cases.title: Clinical Study Protocol Drafting
use_cases.description: Quickly create structured protocol drafts for new clinical trials using PICO inputs. The skill selects the correct template and calculates sample size automatically.
use_cases.target_user: Clinical research coordinators planning new studies

use_cases.title: Medical Writing Assistance
use_cases.description: Generate structured outlines with section-specific writing guidance for study protocols. Each section includes tailored LLM instructions for content generation.

use_cases.title: Diagnostic Study Design
use_cases.description: Design diagnostic accuracy studies with sensitivity and specificity parameters. The skill calculates sample size based on expected diagnostic performance and disease prevalence.
use_cases.target_user: Academic researchers evaluating new diagnostic tests

prompt_templates.title: Efficacy Trial Protocol Outline
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a protocol for an efficacy study of [intervention] in [population] compared to [comparator], measuring [outcome]. Study design: RCT.
prompt_templates.scenario: A researcher needs a full protocol outline for a drug efficacy trial

prompt_templates.title: Diagnostic Accuracy Study
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a diagnostic study protocol for [index test] to detect [condition] using [reference standard]. Report sensitivity, specificity, and required sample size.
prompt_templates.scenario: A clinician wants to evaluate a new diagnostic test

prompt_templates.title: Sample Size Estimation Request
prompt_templates.prompt: Calculate the required sample size for a [study type] study with alpha [value] and power [value]. Population expected event rate is [rate].
prompt_templates.scenario: A researcher needs to determine how many participants to recruit

prompt_templates.title: Custom PICO Protocol Framework
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a complete protocol framework for a [study type] study. Population: [P]. Intervention: [I]. Comparator: [C]. Outcome: [O]. Study design: [design].
prompt_templates.scenario: An advanced user needs a tailored protocol with specific PICO elements

output_examples.input: Design a protocol for an efficacy study of Metformin in Type 2 Diabetes patients with placebo as comparator and HbA1c as outcome.
output_examples.output: The skill generated a structured protocol outline with 10 major sections: title page, synopsis, introduction, objectives, study design, population criteria, interventions, procedures, outcomes, and statistical analysis. Each section contained specific LLM writing instructions shown as blockquotes. The sample size was calculated and inserted into the synopsis and statistical analysis sections.

output_examples.input: Create a diagnostic study for a new rapid COVID-19 antigen test using PCR as the reference standard.
output_examples.output: The skill classified this as a diagnostic accuracy study and selected the STARD-compatible template. It generated sections with guidance on index test description, reference standard protocol, and blinding procedures. The calculated sample size accounted for expected sensitivity (80%), specificity (80%), and prevalence (50%).

best_practices: Define all PICO elements clearly before starting the protocol generation for accurate template selection
Review calculated sample sizes and adjust statistical parameters like alpha and power to match study requirements
Customize the generated LLM instructions and placeholder values for your specific study context and institution
anti_patterns: Do not bypass the structured workflow by asking the LLM to generate content without running the packaged scripts
Do not use placeholder identifiers from the protocol templates without replacing them with study-specific information
Do not treat the generated outline as a final regulatory submission document without expert review

faq.question: What study types does this skill support?
faq.answer: The skill supports diagnostic, efficacy, etiology, and prognosis study types. Each type uses a different protocol template and sample size calculation method.

faq.question: How does sample size calculation work?
faq.answer: The skill uses standard statistical formulas specific to each study type. You provide parameters like alpha, power, and expected event rates, and the skill computes the required sample size.

faq.question: Can this skill replace a biostatistician?
faq.answer: No. The skill provides basic sample size calculations and protocol structure, but it does not replace expert statistical review or ethics committee approval.

faq.question: What output format does the skill produce?
faq.answer: The skill produces a markdown file with organized protocol sections. Each section includes LLM writing instructions marked as blockquotes for content generation.

faq.question: Do I need to install special software?
faq.answer: You need Python 3.10 or higher. No additional third-party packages are required beyond the skill scripts themselves.

faq.question: Can I use this skill for regulatory submissions?
faq.answer: The skill produces planning frameworks and outlines. You should not use the raw output as a regulatory submission document without expert review and customization.
